backend/e-journal/src/main/java/com/edut: This file serves as the entry point for the EduTrack school e-journal system. It defines the EJournalApplication class which is annotated with SpringBootApplication to enable auto-configuration and component scanning. The main method initializes the Spring Boot framework and starts the embedded web server. This setup allows the application to run independently without requiring an external servlet container. It acts as the foundation for loading all other beans and services within the system.

This configuration class sets up the OpenAPI documentation for the EduTrack school e-journal system using Swagger. It defines a global JWT Bearer authentication scheme named bearerAuth that applies to all endpoints except the three authentication endpoints. The OpenAPI bean provides metadata including the API title, version, and detailed descriptions of the five user roles and their permissions. It also includes contact information for the development team to assist with API inquiries. This setup ensures that developers and consumers of the API have clear, standardized documentation for integration and testing.

This configuration class establishes the security framework for the EduTrack school e-journal system by integrating Spring Security with JWT-based authentication. It defines a stateless session policy and disables CSRF protection to support a modern API architecture. The setup exposes essential beans for password encoding and authentication management while configuring a custom filter chain that validates requests via a JWT filter and a local admin filter. Public endpoints for authentication and Swagger documentation are explicitly permitted, whereas all other requests require authentication. Additionally, it configures CORS to allow cross-origin requests from the frontend development server.

This controller manages student absence records within the EduTrack system by exposing RESTful endpoints under the /api/absences path. It handles listing absences for specific classes or students, retrieving personal absence history for authenticated students, and creating new absence entries. The system enforces strict role-based access control, allowing teachers and administrators to create or modify records while restricting student access to their own data. Key operations include recording new absences, toggling their excused status, and permanently deleting records. Each endpoint delegates business logic to the AbsenceService layer and returns standardized DTOs. Swagger annotations are used to document the API for external consumers.

This controller handles user authentication flows for the EduTrack system by exposing endpoints for login, token refresh, and logout. It validates credentials through Spring Security and generates short-lived access tokens along with long-lived refresh tokens stored in HttpOnly cookies. The login endpoint authenticates users and returns an access token while setting a secure refresh cookie for future use. The refresh endpoint allows clients to obtain new access tokens without re-entering credentials by validating the existing refresh cookie. The logout endpoint clears the refresh cookie to terminate the session securely. This design ensures secure stateless authentication while maintaining user sessions via cookie-based token management.

This controller manages HTTP endpoints for querying school classes within the EduTrack system. It exposes REST APIs to retrieve all classes, filter classes by school, fetch specific class details, and list enrolled students. The implementation relies on the ClassService for business logic and uses Spring Security annotations to enforce role-based access control. Different endpoints are restricted to specific user roles such as ADMIN, TEACHER, or PARENT to ensure data privacy. Swagger annotations are included to document the API operations for developers. This component serves as the primary interface for class-related data retrieval in the application.

This controller manages disciplinary complaint records within the EduTrack system by exposing RESTful endpoints under the /api/complaints path. It handles operations for listing complaints by class or specific student, allowing authorized users like teachers and administrators to view records. The system also permits students to retrieve their own complaint history and enables authorized staff to file new complaints or delete existing ones. Access to these endpoints is strictly controlled via role-based authorization to ensure data security. The implementation relies on a dedicated service layer to process business logic and returns standardized DTOs to the client.

This controller manages the RESTful endpoints for recording and querying student grades within the EduTrack system. It exposes endpoints for listing grades by class or student, retrieving a student's own grades, and creating or deleting individual grade records. The implementation relies on the GradeService to handle business logic and uses DTOs for data transfer. Access to these endpoints is strictly controlled by role-based authorization, ensuring that only authorized teachers, parents, admins, or students can perform specific actions. The system supports the Bulgarian grading scale ranging from 2 to 6.

This controller manages administrative operations for parent accounts within the EduTrack system. It exposes REST endpoints that allow headmasters and administrators to list parents, link or unlink them to students, and update parent details. All endpoints require authentication and enforce role-based access control to ensure only authorized personnel can modify school data. The controller delegates business logic to the ParentService layer while handling HTTP request mapping and response formatting. It serves as the primary interface for managing the relationship between parent users and student records in the e-journal application.

This controller manages authenticated users' profile operations within the EduTrack system by exposing REST endpoints under the /api/profile path. It handles retrieving user details, changing passwords, updating biographical information, and managing profile picture uploads or deletions. Each endpoint relies on the injected ProfileService to execute business logic and uses Spring Security to ensure only logged-in users can access their own data. The API supports standard HTTP methods like GET, PUT, and DELETE to facilitate comprehensive profile customization. Swagger annotations are included to document the available operations and expected responses for developers. This component serves as the primary interface for users to maintain their personal account settings and identity information.

This controller manages curriculum scheduling endpoints for the EduTrack system, allowing users to view and modify which teachers teach which subjects to specific classes. It exposes RESTful operations to list schedules by class or teacher, create new assignments, update lecture types, and delete entries. Access control is strictly enforced, restricting creation and modification actions to administrators and headmasters while allowing broader read access for teachers, parents, and students. The implementation relies on a dedicated service layer to handle business logic and data persistence. Swagger annotations are included to document the API for developers.

This controller manages school-related operations within the EduTrack system by exposing REST endpoints under the /api/schools path. It handles core CRUD activities for schools, including listing, retrieving, creating, updating, and deleting school records. The controller enforces strict role-based access control, restricting most administrative actions to users with the ADMIN role while allowing headmasters limited update capabilities. It delegates business logic to the SchoolService layer and utilizes DTOs for data transfer. Additionally, it supports specific operations for updating school information and managing associated profiles and schedules. This component serves as the primary interface for external clients to interact with school data in the e-journal application.

This controller manages student enrollment and expulsion operations within the EduTrack system. It exposes REST endpoints for headmasters to list available students, enroll new ones, or remove existing students from the school. All endpoints are strictly protected, requiring the HEADMASTER role for access. The logic delegates to the StudentService for business processing while returning appropriate HTTP status codes. Swagger annotations document the API for external consumers. This component serves as the primary interface for headmaster student management tasks.

This controller manages HTTP endpoints for school subjects within the EduTrack system. It exposes RESTful operations to list, create, update, and delete subject records. All write operations are restricted to users with the ADMIN role, while listing is available to both ADMIN and HEADMASTER roles. The class delegates business logic to a SubjectService and uses DTOs for data transfer. Swagger annotations are included to document the API for developers.

This controller manages headmaster operations for hiring, firing, and managing teacher salaries within the EduTrack system. It exposes REST endpoints to list teachers by school, view available unassigned teachers, and perform administrative actions like creating new teacher accounts or updating compensation. All endpoints are strictly protected, requiring either ADMIN or HEADMASTER roles to ensure proper authorization. The class delegates business logic to the TeacherService, handling complex tasks such as verifying school ownership and processing user creation. It also integrates Swagger annotations to document the API for external consumers. This component serves as the primary interface for school leadership to oversee staff composition and payroll.

This controller manages user administration endpoints for the EduTrack system, handling CRUD operations and role-based queries. It exposes REST APIs for creating, updating, and deleting user accounts, as well as retrieving lists of users filtered by role such as teachers, students, parents, and headmasters. Access to these endpoints is strictly controlled, with most administrative actions restricted to users with the ADMIN role and certain queries also available to HEADMASTERs. The class delegates business logic to a UserService layer, ensuring separation of concerns while returning standardized DTOs to the client. It also supports retrieving user profile pictures as raw image bytes. This component serves as the primary interface for managing the system's user base and their associated school data.

This file defines a Data Transfer Object for representing student absence records within the EduTrack system. The AbsenceDto class encapsulates essential details such as the student and subject identifiers, names, and the specific date of the absence. It also tracks administrative metadata like the term number and whether the absence has been excused. This structure facilitates the seamless transfer of absence data between the backend service and the frontend interface. By using Lombok annotations, the class automatically generates getters and a constructor to reduce boilerplate code.

This file defines a data transfer object for handling absence requests within the EduTrack system. The AbsenceRequest class encapsulates the essential details required to log a student's absence, including their unique identifier and the specific schedule instance. It also captures the date of the absence in a standardized string format. Validation annotations ensure that the student ID, schedule ID, and date are all provided and not empty. This structure facilitates secure and consistent data transmission from the frontend to the backend services.

This class serves as a data transfer object for returning authentication results to the client. It is a simple record that holds a single string field for the access token. The class uses Lombok annotations to automatically generate the constructor and getter methods. This reduces boilerplate code while ensuring the token is immutable after creation. It plays a key role in the login flow by packaging the generated JWT for immediate client use.

This file defines a data transfer object for handling password change requests within the EduTrack system. The ChangePasswordRequest class encapsulates the current password and the desired new password. It uses Jakarta validation annotations to ensure the current password is not blank and the new password meets a minimum length requirement. The class relies on Lombok to automatically generate getters and a no-argument constructor. This structure ensures secure and validated input for user authentication updates.

This file defines a data transfer object for handling complaint information within the EduTrack system. The ComplaintDto class encapsulates key details such as the complaint identifier, associated student and teacher names, and the specific subject involved. It also captures contextual metadata like the academic term, date of the incident, and a textual description of the issue. By using Lombok annotations, the class automatically generates getters and a constructor to streamline data binding. This structure facilitates the seamless transfer of complaint data between the backend service layer and the frontend interface.

This file defines the ComplaintRequest data transfer object used to capture student complaint submissions. It includes fields for the student identifier, schedule identifier, complaint date, and a detailed description of the issue. The class utilizes Lombok annotations to automatically generate getters and a no-argument constructor, reducing boilerplate code. Jakarta validation constraints ensure that the student ID, schedule ID, date, and description are not null or empty. This structure standardizes the input format for creating new complaints within the EduTrack system.

This file defines a data transfer object for creating new user accounts within the EduTrack system. It utilizes Jakarta validation annotations to enforce strict input constraints on fields like first name, last name, email, and password. The class also captures the user role, which determines access levels such as admin or teacher. Lombok annotations are used to automatically generate getters and a default constructor, reducing boilerplate code. This object serves as the structured input payload for user registration endpoints.

This class defines a data transfer object for representing individual student grades within the system. It encapsulates essential details such as the grade identifier, student and subject information, and the specific term. The value field stores the formatted grade result while the timestamp records when the entry was created. By using Lombok annotations, it automatically generates constructors and getters to simplify data handling. This structure facilitates the transfer of grade data between the backend service and the frontend interface.

This class defines the data structure for submitting a new grade entry within the EduTrack system. It utilizes Lombok annotations to automatically generate getters and a no-argument constructor, ensuring clean and concise code. The model enforces strict validation rules to guarantee data integrity during the submission process. Specifically, it requires non-null identifiers for both the student and the specific class schedule. The grade value is constrained to a BigDecimal type, restricted to a range between 2.00 and 6.00 to match the institution's grading scale. This DTO serves as the primary input contract for the backend service handling grade updates.

This class defines the data structure for user login requests within the EduTrack system. It contains two mandatory fields, email and password, which are validated to ensure the email format is correct and neither field is empty. The use of Lombok annotations automatically generates getters and a no-argument constructor to simplify code. This DTO serves as the input payload for authentication endpoints, ensuring data integrity before processing credentials. It plays a critical role in the secure entry point for users accessing the e-journal platform.

This file defines a data transfer object for representing a parent user within the EduTrack system. It encapsulates core identity details such as the parent's ID, name, and email address. The class also includes a nested static class to list the associated students, capturing each child's ID and name. This structure facilitates the transfer of parent and child information between the backend and frontend layers. It is primarily used to display family relationships in the e-journal interface.

This file defines a data transfer object for managing class schedules within the EduTrack system. The ScheduleDto class encapsulates essential details such as subject, teacher, and class identifiers along with their corresponding names. It also stores temporal information like the term, day of the week, and start and end times in HH:mm format. Additional fields capture the lecture type and whether attendance tracking is enabled for the session. This structure facilitates the seamless transfer of schedule data between the backend services and the frontend interface.

This file defines a data transfer object used to capture schedule creation or update requests within the EduTrack system. It includes mandatory identifiers for the class, subject, and teacher, along with validation constraints for the term and day of the week. The object also stores the start and end times for the lecture in a standard hour-minute format. Optional fields allow specifying the lecture type and whether attendance should be tracked for extracurricular activities. This structure ensures that incoming schedule data is properly validated before being processed by the backend service.

This file defines a data transfer object for representing school class information within the EduTrack system. The SchoolClassDto class encapsulates key attributes such as the class identifier, name, associated school year, and details about the parent school. It uses Lombok annotations to automatically generate constructors and getters, reducing boilerplate code. This structure facilitates the transfer of class data between different layers of the application, particularly when displaying class details alongside school context. It serves as a lightweight model for API responses or internal service communication.

This file defines a data transfer object for representing school information within the EduTrack system. The primary SchoolDto class encapsulates core school attributes such as identifier, name, address, type, and headmaster details. It also includes a nullable student limit and a list of associated profile objects. The nested ProfileDto class provides a simplified view of user profiles linked to the school. This structure facilitates efficient data exchange between the backend service layer and the frontend interface.

This file defines a data transfer object for creating or updating school records within the EduTrack system. The SchoolRequest class encapsulates essential school details such as the name, address, headmaster identifier, and school type. It utilizes Jakarta validation annotations to ensure the school name is provided and does not exceed a specific character limit. The class relies on Lombok to automatically generate getters and a default constructor, reducing boilerplate code. This DTO serves as the input structure for API endpoints that manage school information.

This file defines a data transfer object for representing individual entries within a school schedule. The SchoolScheduleEntryDto class encapsulates essential details such as the entry identifier, type, label, and time range. It also includes an optional event date for special occasions and a sort order to maintain correct display sequence. By using Lombok annotations, the class automatically generates getters and a constructor to reduce boilerplate code. This DTO serves as a lightweight structure for transmitting schedule data between the backend and frontend components of the EduTrack system.

This class defines the data transfer object for creating or updating school schedule entries within the EduTrack system. It captures essential details such as the entry type, a descriptive label, and the start and end times formatted as HH:mm strings. The optional eventDate field allows for specific date assignment, while the sortOrder field supports manual or automatic ordering of schedule items. Validation constraints ensure that critical fields like type and label are not blank and that the label length remains manageable. This structure facilitates clean data exchange between the frontend and backend services for managing daily school activities.

This file defines a data transfer object for managing school term configuration settings within the EduTrack system. The SchoolTermConfigDto class encapsulates specific date strings that determine the start and end dates for various academic terms and school levels. It uses Lombok annotations to automatically generate constructors and getters, simplifying the code structure. The dates are formatted as month-day pairs to indicate key milestones for elementary, progymnasium, and gymnasium students. This DTO facilitates the transfer of term configuration data between the backend service and the frontend interface.

This file defines a simple data transfer object for subject information within the EduTrack system. The SubjectDto class encapsulates a unique identifier and the corresponding subject name. It utilizes Lombok annotations to automatically generate getters and a constructor, reducing boilerplate code. This DTO serves as a lightweight container for transferring subject details between the backend service and other system components. Its minimal structure ensures efficient data exchange without exposing internal entity logic.

This class defines a data transfer object for creating or updating subject information within the EduTrack system. It uses Lombok annotations to automatically generate getters and a no-argument constructor, reducing boilerplate code. The name field is mandatory and limited to one hundred characters to ensure data integrity. Validation constraints enforce that the subject name is not blank and fits within the specified size limit. This object serves as the input structure for API endpoints handling subject management.

This file defines a data transfer object for teacher information within the EduTrack system. The TeacherDto class encapsulates core attributes such as identifier, name, email, salary, and the number of classes taught. It also includes a nested SubjectItem class to represent the list of subjects a teacher is qualified to teach. Both classes use Lombok annotations to automatically generate getters and constructors, reducing boilerplate code. This structure facilitates the transfer of teacher data between the application layers without exposing internal entity details.

This class defines the data structure for updating user information within the EduTrack system. It includes required fields for first name, last name, email, and role, all of which are validated using Jakarta annotations to ensure data integrity. The email field specifically requires a valid format and non-blank content. The role field is mandatory and must not be null. The password field is optional, allowing users to update other details without changing their credentials. This DTO facilitates secure and consistent user profile modifications across the application.

This file defines the UserDto class to represent user data within the EduTrack system. It uses Lombok annotations to automatically generate getters and a constructor for all fields. The class stores essential user details such as name, email, role, and bio. It also includes optional school-specific information like schoolId and schoolName which are null for admins and parents. This structure facilitates data transfer between the backend and frontend layers.

This file defines a simple data transfer object for carrying minimal user information across system boundaries. The UserSummaryDto class holds only the user's unique identifier and display name. It uses Lombok annotations to automatically generate the constructor and getter methods, reducing boilerplate code. This structure is typically used in API responses where full user details are unnecessary. It helps keep network payloads lightweight by excluding sensitive or redundant data.

This file defines the Absence entity to track when a student misses a specific scheduled session. It maps to the absences database table and stores the student, schedule, date, and excusal status for each record. The class uses JPA annotations to establish relationships with Student and Schedule entities via lazy loading. Lombok annotations simplify boilerplate code by generating getters, setters, constructors, and a builder pattern. This entity serves as the core data model for recording attendance deviations within the EduTrack system.

This file defines the Complaint entity which models behavioral infractions reported by teachers against students. It links a specific student to a particular schedule entry and includes a mandatory description and the date of the incident. The class uses JPA annotations to map to a database table and Lombok annotations to reduce boilerplate code. This entity serves as the core data structure for tracking student discipline records within the EduTrack system.

The Grade entity represents a specific score awarded to a student for a particular schedule entry within the EduTrack system. It utilizes the Bulgarian grading scale, restricting values to a range between 2.00 and 6.00. The class maintains relationships with both the Student and Schedule entities to link grades to the correct academic context. It automatically records the timestamp of creation when a new grade is persisted. This structure ensures data integrity and accurate tracking of student performance over time.

This file defines an enumeration that categorizes the different types of lectures within the EduTrack system. It includes three distinct values: STANDARD for whole-class sessions, SIP for specialized elective subjects involving mixed student groups, and EXTRACURRICULAR for optional activities that may span various age levels. This enum serves as a foundational data type for distinguishing lecture formats in the database and application logic. By standardizing these categories, it ensures consistent handling of attendance and scheduling rules across the e-journal platform.

This file defines the Role entity which maps to the roles database table in the EduTrack system. It uses JPA annotations to manage persistence and Lombok annotations to automatically generate boilerplate code like getters and setters. The class contains a unique identifier and a name field that stores the role type as a string. This entity serves as the foundational data structure for managing user permissions and access levels within the application.

This file defines an enumeration that establishes the distinct user roles within the EduTrack school e-journal system. It lists five specific roles: ADMIN, HEADMASTER, TEACHER, STUDENT, and PARENT. These values serve as the foundational identifiers for access control and permission management across the application. By centralizing these role definitions, the system ensures consistent user classification throughout the backend logic. This approach simplifies security checks and role-based feature availability for different user types.

The Schedule entity models the core timetable structure by linking a specific class, subject, and teacher for a given term. It captures essential scheduling details such as the day of the week, start and end times, and the type of lecture. The system supports three lecture types: STANDARD, SIP, and EXTRACURRICULAR, with attendance tracking enabled by default for the first two. This entity serves as the foundational reference for recording student grades and absences within the EduTrack e-journal. By enforcing constraints on terms and days, it ensures data integrity for the school's academic calendar.

This file defines an enumeration that categorizes different types of schedule entries within the system. It includes three specific values: LECTURE for standard classes, BREAK for rest periods, and SPECIAL_EVENT for unique activities. This enum serves as a foundational data type for modeling daily timetables. It ensures consistent classification of time slots across the application. By using this enum, the system maintains data integrity for schedule-related features.

This file defines the School entity which maps to the schools database table in the EduTrack system. It uses JPA annotations to manage persistence and Lombok to automatically generate boilerplate code like getters and setters. The class stores essential school details such as name, address, type, and an optional student enrollment limit. It also maintains a lazy-loaded relationship with a User entity representing the school director. This entity serves as the core data model for managing school information within the application.

This file defines the SchoolClass entity which represents a specific group of students within a school for a particular academic year. It maps to the classes table in the database and includes fields for a unique identifier, a short name, and the school year formatted as a string. The entity establishes a many-to-one relationship with the School entity to link each class to its parent institution. Validation constraints ensure the name and school year are not blank and adhere to length limits. This class serves as a fundamental data model for organizing students by their respective classes and academic terms in the EduTrack system.

This file defines the SchoolProfile entity which serves as a bridge between the application's core School entity and user-facing profile data. It uses JPA annotations to map the class to the school_profiles database table and manages the relationship with the School entity via a many-to-one association. The class includes standard Lombok annotations to automatically generate getters, setters, constructors, and builder methods, reducing boilerplate code. Validation constraints ensure the profile name is present and does not exceed one hundred characters. This structure allows the system to store and retrieve specific profile details for each school while maintaining referential integrity.

This file defines the SchoolScheduleEntry entity which represents a specific time block or event within a school's daily schedule. It maps to the school_schedule_entries table and includes fields for the entry type, a descriptive label, start and end times, and a sort order for display purposes. The entity supports two main modes: recurring daily entries defined by time ranges and special events that include a specific date. It maintains a lazy-loaded relationship with the School entity to associate each schedule item with a specific institution. This class serves as the foundational data model for managing and displaying school timetables in the EduTrack system.

This entity defines per-school configuration for term and school-year date boundaries within the EduTrack system. It maintains a one-to-one relationship with the School entity, sharing the same primary key to ensure each school has exactly one configuration record. The class stores key dates as MM-dd strings, including the start of the school year and the start of the second term. It also specifies distinct end dates for different grade levels, such as elementary, progymnasium, and gymnasium. This configuration allows the system to dynamically calculate academic periods based on individual school policies.

This file defines an enumeration that categorizes the various types of schools within the EduTrack system. It includes specific values such as GENERAL, FOREIGN_LANGUAGE, MATHEMATICS, ART, SPORTS, and PROFESSIONAL to represent distinct educational focuses. By using an enum, the system ensures type safety and consistency when storing or querying school classifications in the database. This approach prevents invalid school types from being entered into the system and simplifies business logic that depends on school categories. It serves as a foundational reference for identifying the specialized nature of each institution in the e-journal application.

This file defines the Student entity which represents a student user within the EduTrack system. It extends the base User entity by linking to specific school, class, and profile details through one-to-one and many-to-one relationships. The primary key is shared with the associated User record to maintain referential integrity. Optional fields allow for flexible enrollment states where class or profile assignments might not yet be established. This structure supports the core data model for tracking student academic progress and administrative assignments.

This file defines the Subject entity for the EduTrack school e-journal system. It maps to the subjects database table and stores unique subject names with a maximum length of one hundred characters. The class uses JPA annotations to manage persistence and Lombok annotations to automatically generate boilerplate code like getters and setters. This entity serves as a foundational data model for organizing academic content within the application.

This file defines the Teacher entity which extends the base User model to represent educators within the EduTrack system. It uses a one-to-one relationship with the User table to share the primary key while adding specific teacher-related data. The entity links each teacher to a specific school and stores their monthly gross salary in Bulgarian Lev. Additionally, it maintains a many-to-many relationship with the Subject table to track the teacher's professional qualifications. This structure allows the system to manage teacher profiles, assignments, and compensation efficiently.

This file defines the User entity which maps to the users table in the database. It stores core authentication and profile data including email, password hash, name, and role. The class uses JPA annotations to handle database persistence and Lombok to reduce boilerplate code. It supports role-based access control through a mandatory relationship with the Role entity. Profile images are stored as binary data with their MIME type for proper rendering. This entity serves as the foundation for user identity and security within the EduTrack system.

This interface defines the data access layer for managing student absence records within the EduTrack system. It extends Spring Data JPA to provide standard database operations for the Absence entity. The implementation includes custom query methods to filter absences by either a specific school class or a particular student. These queries leverage JPA's relationship traversal to efficiently locate relevant records without manual SQL coding. This component serves as the bridge between the application logic and the underlying database for attendance tracking.

This interface defines the data access layer for the SchoolClass entity within the EduTrack system. It extends Spring Data JPA's JpaRepository to provide standard database operations like saving and finding by ID. The custom method findAllBySchool_Id allows the application to retrieve all classes belonging to a specific school using its unique identifier. This functionality is essential for organizing student and teacher data by school affiliation. It serves as a bridge between the business logic and the underlying database for class management.

This interface defines the data access layer for managing complaints within the EduTrack system. It extends Spring Data JPA to provide standard database operations for the Complaint entity. The code includes custom query methods to filter complaints by specific school classes or individual students. These functions allow the application to efficiently retrieve relevant complaint records based on class schedules or student identities. This repository serves as the bridge between the business logic and the underlying database storage.

This interface defines data access operations for the Grade entity within the EduTrack system. It extends Spring Data JPA to provide standard database persistence capabilities for grade records. The implementation includes a method to fetch all grades linked to a specific school class by its ID. Another method allows retrieving all grades for a particular student using their unique identifier. These functions enable the application to query academic performance data efficiently for both class-level and student-level views.

This interface defines the data access layer for managing user roles within the EduTrack system. It extends Spring Data's CrudRepository to provide standard database operations for the Role entity. The primary custom method retrieves a specific role by its enum name, ensuring type-safe lookups. This component is essential for authentication and authorization processes that determine user permissions. It acts as a bridge between the application logic and the underlying database storage for role definitions.

This interface defines the data access layer for managing educational schedules within the EduTrack system. It extends Spring Data JPA to provide standard database operations for the Schedule entity. The interface includes custom query methods to fetch schedules filtered by either a specific school class or a teacher. These queries allow the application to efficiently retrieve timetable data based on user roles. This component serves as the bridge between the business logic and the underlying database storage.

This interface defines the data access layer for school profile entities within the EduTrack system. It extends Spring Data JPA's JpaRepository to provide standard database operations for the SchoolProfile entity. The custom method findAllBySchool_Id allows querying profiles linked to a specific school identifier. This component serves as the bridge between the application logic and the underlying database storage. It ensures efficient retrieval and management of school profile records.

This interface defines the data access layer for the School entity within the EduTrack system. It extends Spring Data's JpaRepository to provide standard CRUD operations for school records. A custom query method is included to locate a school record based on the ID of its associated director. This functionality supports user authentication and authorization by linking school data to specific administrative users. The use of Optional ensures safe handling of cases where no school is found for a given director.

This interface defines the data access layer for managing school schedule entries within the EduTrack system. It extends Spring Data JPA to provide standard database operations for the SchoolScheduleEntry entity. The primary custom method retrieves all schedule items for a specific school, ensuring they are returned in the correct lecture order. This functionality supports the e-journal feature by allowing the application to fetch and display a complete weekly timetable efficiently.

This interface defines the data access layer for managing school term configurations within the EduTrack system. It extends Spring Data's JpaRepository to provide standard CRUD operations for the SchoolTermConfig entity. By leveraging Spring Data JPA, it automatically implements persistence logic without requiring manual code. This allows the application to store and retrieve term settings such as start and end dates. The repository serves as a bridge between the business logic and the underlying database. It ensures consistent and efficient handling of term configuration data.

This interface defines data access operations for the Student entity within the EduTrack system. It extends Spring Data JPA to provide standard persistence capabilities while adding custom queries for specific business needs. Key methods allow filtering students by school, class, parent, or null school assignments. These functions support features like roster management and parent-student linking. The repository acts as the bridge between the application logic and the database for student records.

This file defines the data access layer for the Subject entity within the EduTrack system. It declares a Java interface named SubjectRepository that extends Spring Data's JpaRepository. By inheriting from JpaRepository, this interface automatically provides standard CRUD operations for managing Subject records in the database. The generic types specify that the repository handles Subject entities identified by Long IDs. This abstraction allows the service layer to interact with the database without writing explicit SQL queries. It serves as a foundational component for storing and retrieving subject information in the e-journal application.

This interface defines data access operations for the Teacher entity within the EduTrack system. It extends Spring Data's JpaRepository to provide standard CRUD capabilities for teacher records. The findAllBySchool_Id method allows querying all teachers belonging to a specific school using its identifier. The findAllBySchoolIsNull method retrieves teachers who are not yet assigned to any school, supporting potential hiring workflows. Together, these functions enable efficient management of teacher assignments and unassigned staff in the e-journal application.

This interface defines the data access layer for user management within the EduTrack system by extending Spring Data's CrudRepository. It provides standard methods to locate users by email and verify email uniqueness to prevent duplicate accounts. The repository also supports role-based queries to retrieve all users assigned to a specific security role. Two custom JPQL queries identify available students and teachers who have not yet been enrolled or assigned to a school. This functionality is critical for the onboarding process, allowing the system to efficiently find unassigned users for new school enrollments.

This service implements Spring Security's UserDetailsService interface to handle user authentication for the EduTrack system. It retrieves user accounts from the database using the email address provided during login. The implementation maps the domain User entity to Spring Security's UserDetails object, ensuring the correct password hash and role are loaded. It converts the user's role into a GrantedAuthority with the appropriate ROLE_ prefix for authorization checks. If no user is found with the given email, it throws a UsernameNotFoundException to indicate invalid credentials. This class serves as the bridge between the application's data layer and the security framework.

This component intercepts incoming HTTP requests to validate JSON Web Tokens for the EduTrack school e-journal system. It extracts the Bearer token from the Authorization header and verifies its signature and expiration using JwtUtils. Upon successful validation, it retrieves the user's email and loads their details from the database to establish a Spring Security context. The filter logs detailed audit information including timestamps, IP addresses, and user roles for both successful and failed authentication attempts. If the token is invalid or the user is not found, the request proceeds without authentication but with a warning log. This mechanism ensures that only authenticated users with valid credentials can access protected resources in the application.

This component manages JSON Web Token operations for the EduTrack system by generating and validating access and refresh tokens. It creates access tokens containing user details like ID, email, and role, while refresh tokens are shorter-lived and contain only the subject. The class uses a configured secret key to cryptographically sign these tokens to ensure their integrity and authenticity. It provides methods to extract the user email from a token and to validate whether a token is still active and properly signed. This utility ensures secure authentication flows by handling token lifecycle management within the application's security layer.

This filter provides a development convenience by automatically granting full administrative privileges to requests originating from localhost when the allow-localhost flag is enabled. It checks if the request comes from a loopback address and lacks an existing authentication token. If both conditions are met, it creates a new authentication object with all system roles, effectively bypassing standard security checks. This mechanism simplifies local testing by allowing developers to access protected endpoints without valid JWTs. The feature is explicitly disabled in staging and production environments to maintain security integrity. It is registered directly in the security configuration rather than as a standard Spring component.

This component implements a servlet filter that logs details for every incoming HTTP request in the EduTrack system. It extends OncePerRequestFilter to ensure exactly one execution per request and uses SLF4J to record the HTTP method, URI, response status, processing time, and client IP address. The filter captures the start time before delegating to the next filter in the chain and logs the duration after the response is generated. It also handles proxy headers to accurately identify the original client IP address. As a high-priority component, it provides essential visibility into request flow and performance for debugging and monitoring purposes.

The AbsenceService manages the lifecycle of student absence records within the EduTrack system. It provides methods to retrieve absences by class, authenticated student, or specific student ID, converting database entities into DTOs for API responses. The service handles creating new absence entries by validating associated student and schedule data before persistence. It also supports toggling the excused status of existing records and deleting them by ID. A helper method resolves authenticated users from security principals to ensure proper access control. This service acts as the core business logic layer for attendance tracking operations.

ClassService manages the retrieval and mapping of school class data for the EduTrack system. It provides methods to fetch all classes, classes by school, or a specific class by ID, ensuring proper authorization checks for administrators and headmasters. The service also retrieves student lists associated with a given class. It relies on Spring Data repositories to access SchoolClass, Student, and User entities from the database. Internal helper methods convert entity objects into Data Transfer Objects for API responses. This service acts as a central business logic layer for class-related operations within the application.

This service manages the lifecycle of behavioral complaints within the EduTrack system by handling creation, retrieval, and deletion operations. It exposes methods to fetch complaints for specific classes or individual students, ensuring data is properly filtered and mapped to DTOs for API responses. The create function validates that both the student and schedule exist before persisting a new complaint record to the database. It also includes helper methods to resolve authenticated users and flatten entity relationships into simple data transfer objects. This component acts as the core business logic layer for tracking student conduct issues linked to specific classes and teachers.

This service manages the lifecycle of academic grades within the EduTrack system, handling retrieval, creation, and deletion operations. It enforces strict validation by ensuring all new grades conform to a specific Bulgarian grading scale before persisting them to the database. The implementation supports fetching grades by class, current authenticated student, or specific student ID, mapping complex entity relationships into simplified DTOs for API responses. It also securely resolves user identities from Spring Security principals to associate grades with the correct students and teachers. By leveraging Spring Data repositories, it efficiently queries and updates grade records while maintaining data integrity through existence checks.

ParentService manages the lifecycle of parent accounts and their associations with students within the EduTrack system. It provides methods to retrieve parents linked to a specific school, fetch all available parent users, and handle the linking or unlinking of students to parents. The service enforces security by verifying that the authenticated headmaster has authority over the relevant school and students before performing any modifications. It also supports updating parent profile details such as name and email. By grouping students under their respective parents, it facilitates efficient data retrieval for the school administration interface.

ProfileService manages user profile operations within the EduTrack system by handling authentication context through Spring Security principals. It provides methods to retrieve user details, securely update passwords with validation, and manage profile picture uploads or deletions. The service also allows users to update their biography with length constraints to ensure data integrity. Each operation validates input and persists changes to the database via the UserRepository. This service acts as a central point for modifying user account metadata and credentials. It ensures that all profile updates are tied to the currently authenticated user.

The ScheduleService manages the lifecycle of class schedules within the EduTrack system by handling creation, retrieval, updating, and deletion operations. It interacts with multiple repositories to validate associated classes, subjects, and teachers before persisting new schedule entries or modifying existing ones. The service provides specialized methods to fetch schedules for specific classes, individual teachers, or the currently authenticated user. It also supports partial updates to lecture types and attendance tracking settings without requiring full entity replacement. Internal helper methods map database entities to flattened DTOs to simplify frontend consumption and avoid deep object nesting. This service acts as the core business logic layer for all schedule-related data processing in the application.

This service manages the lifecycle of school entities and their associated profiles within the EduTrack system. It handles core CRUD operations for schools, including creation, updates, and deletion, while enforcing access control for headmasters. The service also manages school profiles by retrieving, adding, and updating them in relation to specific schools. Additionally, it processes school schedules and term configurations to support the e-journal's temporal structure. All data transformations between entities and DTOs are centralized within this class to maintain consistency. This service acts as the primary business logic layer for school-related operations in the application.

The StudentService class manages the lifecycle of student enrollments within the EduTrack system, specifically handling the processes of adding and removing students from schools. It provides methods to retrieve a list of available students who are not currently enrolled and to enroll a user by linking them to the headmaster's school. The service also supports expelling students by clearing their school association after verifying that the requesting headmaster has authority over that specific student. Internal helper methods ensure that the authenticated user is a valid headmaster and that they are associated with a school before performing any enrollment or expulsion actions. This service acts as the business logic layer for student-school relationships, enforcing role-based access control and data integrity.

SubjectService manages the lifecycle of subject entities within the EduTrack system by handling database operations through a dedicated repository. It provides methods to retrieve all subjects, create new ones, update existing records, and delete them based on unique identifiers. The service ensures data integrity by throwing appropriate exceptions when requested subjects are not found. It acts as a bridge between the persistence layer and the API layer by converting database entities into lightweight DTOs for external communication. This class is essential for maintaining the core subject data structure used across the school e-journal application.

The TeacherService class manages the lifecycle of teacher accounts and their association with schools within the EduTrack system. It provides methods for headmasters to hire new teachers, assign existing users to schools, and terminate employment by removing school links. The service enforces strict access controls to ensure only authorized headmasters can modify teacher records for their specific institutions. It handles data validation by checking for duplicate emails and preventing teachers from being assigned to multiple schools simultaneously. Additionally, it supports retrieving teacher lists and updating salary information while maintaining data integrity through repository interactions.

The UserService class manages user lifecycle operations and data retrieval for the EduTrack school e-journal system. It handles creating, updating, and fetching user profiles while ensuring email uniqueness and secure password encoding. The service provides specialized methods to retrieve teachers, students, parents, and headmasters filtered by school or role. It also supports profile picture retrieval by returning image bytes with appropriate content types. This service acts as the central business logic layer for user-related interactions within the application.

frontend/src/context/AuthProvider.jsx: This file provides global authentication state management for the EduTrack system using React Context. It defines the AuthProvider component which exposes login, logout, and token refresh capabilities to the entire application tree. The login function authenticates users via the backend and updates the global state with the JWT token and decoded user profile. A background timer automatically refreshes the access token every fifteen minutes to maintain session continuity. frontend/src/context/SidebarContext.jsx: This file establishes a global context for managing the sidebar's visibility state within the EduTrack application. frontend/src/context/StudentContext.jsx: This file manages the global state for student profiles within the EduTrack system, specifically supporting parents who may have multiple children. A custom useStudent hook is exported to allow any component in the application tree to easily access the current list of students and the active selection. frontend/src/hooks/useAuth.jsx: This file exports a custom React hook that simplifies access to the global authentication state within the EduTrack application. frontend/src/i18n/index.js: This file initializes the internationalization engine for the EduTrack application using i18next and react-i18next. The configuration ensures that the application falls back to English for missing translations and disables HTML escaping for interpolated values. It also listens for language changes to persist the user's selection in the browser's local storage. This setup allows the school e-journal system to dynamically switch between supported languages while maintaining user preferences across sessions.

frontend/src/pages/Statistics/TeacherSta: This file defines a React component named TeacherStatistics that serves as a placeholder for displaying aggregated teacher data within the EduTrack system. It currently renders a minimal div element with static text, indicating that the actual statistical visualization logic has not yet been implemented. As part of the school e-journal application, this component is intended to provide educators with insights into their performance metrics or class statistics. The code structure follows standard React conventions by exporting the functional component as the default module. Future development will likely involve integrating data fetching and charting libraries to replace the current static content.
frontend/src/pages/Student/StudentHome.j: This component serves as the primary landing page for students within the EduTrack system. It renders a standard application layout to ensure consistent navigation and structure across the interface. Inside the layout, it displays a WelcomeBanner component to greet the user and provide immediate context. The file exports this functional component as the default module for routing purposes. Its main role is to act as a simple entry point that delegates visual content to reusable sub-components.
frontend/src/pages/Teacher/TeacherHome.j: This file defines the TeacherHome component, which serves as the landing page for teachers within the EduTrack system. It renders a standard Layout wrapper to ensure consistent navigation and structure across the application. Inside this layout, it displays a WelcomeBanner component to greet the user and provide contextual information. The component is exported as the default module for use in the application's routing configuration. Its primary role is to provide a clean, welcoming entry point for the teacher dashboard.
frontend/src/pages/Teacher/TeacherSchedu: This component renders the teacher's class schedule within the EduTrack system, filtering entries to show only those active in the current academic term. It fetches schedule data and school-specific term configuration from the backend API upon mounting. The logic calculates the active term and validates each entry against grade-level end dates to ensure accuracy. It displays a loading spinner while fetching data and handles errors or empty states with appropriate user feedback. The interface uses Material UI components to present the schedule in a clean table format.
frontend/src/pages/Teacher/TeacherSchool: This component renders the school information and daily schedule for the teacher's view within the EduTrack system. It fetches the current user's school details and associated schedule entries from the backend API upon initial load. The interface displays school metadata such as name, address, type, and headmaster name using Material UI typography components. It also presents the daily schedule in a table format, utilizing chips to visually distinguish between lectures, breaks, and special events. Error states and loading indicators are handled to ensure a smooth user experience during data retrieval.
frontend/src/pages/Unauthorized/Unauthor: This component serves as a fallback interface for handling unauthorized access attempts within the EduTrack system. It is a simple React function that renders a minimal div containing the text Unauthorized. The primary purpose is to provide immediate visual feedback when a user lacks the necessary permissions to view a specific resource. By exporting this component as the default module, it can be easily integrated into routing logic or error boundaries. This ensures a consistent user experience during permission denied scenarios without requiring complex state management.
frontend/src/router/AppRouter.jsx: This file configures the client-side routing for the EduTrack application using React Router. It defines URL paths for all major user roles, including administrators, headmasters, teachers, parents, and students. Each route is wrapped in a ProtectedRoute component to enforce role-based access control before rendering the corresponding page component. The system also handles public access for login and unauthorized states, while redirecting unauthenticated users to the appropriate landing page. This centralizes navigation logic and ensures secure, role-specific content delivery throughout the school e-journal system.
frontend/src/router/ProtectedRoute.jsx: This component acts as a security guard for specific routes within the EduTrack application by verifying user authentication and authorization before rendering content. It utilizes a custom useAuth hook to retrieve the current user's data and checks if the authentication process is still in progress. If the system is loading, it displays a circular progress indicator to provide visual feedback to the user. Once loading completes, it checks if the user is logged in and redirects them to the login page if they are not. If the user is logged in but lacks the required role specified in the props, they are redirected to an unauthorized page. Otherwise, it renders the protected child components, ensuring only authorized personnel can access sensitive parts of the school e-journal system.
frontend/src/router/RoleRedirect.jsx: This component acts as a dynamic router guard within the EduTrack system to direct users to their specific dashboards based on their assigned roles. It retrieves the current user's session data via the useAuth hook and immediately redirects unauthenticated visitors to the login page. For logged-in users, it maps their specific role (such as admin, teacher, or student) to a corresponding dashboard route. The component leverages React Router's Navigate element to execute these redirects seamlessly. This ensures that each user type lands on the appropriate interface immediately after authentication.
frontend/src/styles/global.css: This file establishes foundational reset styles to ensure consistent rendering across different browsers. It applies a border-box model to all elements for predictable sizing calculations. The styles remove default margins and padding from the root elements while forcing full viewport height. This setup ensures the main application container fills the screen properly. It serves as the base layer for the EduTrack school e-journal system's visual layout.
frontend/src/styles/login.css: This stylesheet defines the visual properties for the logo displayed on the EduTrack login page. The primary class, login-logo, ensures the logo maintains its aspect ratio while scaling to a height of 15 percent of the viewport. A fixed margin of 20 pixels is applied to provide consistent spacing around the image element. This styling contributes to a clean and responsive user interface for the school e-journal system. It ensures the branding remains visible and properly positioned regardless of the screen size.
frontend/src/styles/sidebar.css: This stylesheet defines the visual properties for the logo displayed within the application's sidebar navigation. The primary class, sidebar-logo, ensures the logo maintains its aspect ratio while scaling to occupy fifteen percent of the viewport height. It includes a twenty-pixel margin to provide adequate spacing from the surrounding interface elements. This styling is essential for the EduTrack school e-journal system to maintain a consistent and professional brand presence across different screen sizes. By using viewport height units, the logo remains responsive and appropriately sized regardless of the device used.
frontend/src/theme.jsx: This file defines the global design system for the EduTrack application using MUI's createTheme utility. It establishes a custom color palette with a primary blue and a light off-white secondary color to ensure visual consistency across the interface. The configuration includes specific style overrides for input fields, labels, icons, and list items to enforce the dark mode aesthetic. These overrides ensure that text and borders remain visible against the dark background by using light-colored contrasts. The resulting theme object is exported as the default module to be consumed by the rest of the frontend codebase.
sql/create-tables.sql: This script initializes the database schema for the EduTrack school e-journal system by defining core entities such as Users, Roles, Schools, Teachers, and Students. It establishes critical relationships through foreign keys, linking teachers to schools, students to classes, and teachers to their qualifications. The structure supports academic operations by creating tables for Subjects, Schedules, Grades, Absences, and Complaints. Each table includes specific constraints and cascading delete rules to maintain data integrity across the system. This foundation enables the application to manage school administration, class assignments, and student performance tracking effectively.
sql/insert-demo-data.sql: This script populates the EduTrack database with initial demo data for testing and demonstration purposes. It begins by truncating all existing tables to ensure a clean state before inserting new records. The script defines five user roles including admin, headmaster, teacher, student, and parent. It then creates twenty-six user accounts with BCrypt hashed passwords for various staff and student personas across multiple schools. Finally, it establishes the foundational entities such as schools, classes, and schedules to support the school e-journal system. This setup allows developers to quickly verify system functionality without manual data entry.
